products.menuLine: Hendrick's Gin · Scotland · $35 · 88 proof (44.0% ABV) · Notes: cucumber, rose

guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants gin beside a mild cigar — cucumber, rose, and juniper.
guidance.memberBlurb: Hendrick's — cucumber and rose over juniper. The gin for a Connecticut cigar and a martini without fighting the smoke.

products.menuLine: Hennessy VS · Cognac, France · $42 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: grape, oak

guidance.bestFor: Hennessy name at VS tier, cognac-curious, hip-hop/culture recognition.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when Hennessy is the ask at entry tier — grape, almond, and oak before VSOP pricing.
guidance.memberBlurb: Hennessy VS — the cognac tier America buys in volume. Grape and almond over French oak when the member wants the Hennessy name without XO money.

products.menuLine: Hennessy VSOP Privilège · Cognac, France · $55 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, apple

guidance.bestFor: Hennessy name at accessible tier, celebration, medium-full cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when Hennessy is the ask but XO is over budget — honey, apple, almond.
guidance.memberBlurb: Hennessy VSOP — honey and apple from the house everyone knows. Cognac prestige before the XO price jump.

products.menuLine: Hennessy XO · Cognac, France · $200 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: dried fruit, dark chocolate

guidance.bestFor: Cognac request, hip-hop/lounge culture prestige, after-dinner cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants unaged spirits or Scotch only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when cognac prestige is cultural shorthand — dried fruit, chocolate, and oak depth.
guidance.memberBlurb: Hennessy XO — the cognac everyone pictures. Dried fruit, dark chocolate, and oak in a bottle built for celebration.

products.menuLine: Hibiki Japanese Harmony · Japan · $95 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, floral

guidance.bestFor: Luxury Japanese request, elegant pairing, member wants floral-honey profile.
guidance.avoidIf: User demands single malt only or heavy peat.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants Japanese whisky elegance — honey, floral, and polished oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Hibiki Harmony — honey, floral lift, and sandalwood polish. Japanese whisky luxury that photographs as well as it pours.

products.menuLine: Highland Park 12 Year · Orkney, Scotland · $55 · 86 proof (43.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, heather

guidance.bestFor: Peat-curious beginner, floral-honey profile, medium cigar.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member fears peat but wants to explore — heather, honey, and gentle smoke.
guidance.memberBlurb: Highland Park 12 — heather honey with a whisper of Orkney smoke. The bridge pour between Speyside sweetness and Islay fire.

products.menuLine: Jameson Black Barrel · Ireland · $35 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: vanilla, butterscotch

guidance.bestFor: Irish beginner, Jameson name recognition, medium cigar.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants single malt Scotch only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend as approachable Irish — vanilla, butterscotch, and charred oak.
guidance.memberBlurb: Jameson Black Barrel — vanilla and butterscotch from double-charred barrels. Irish whiskey for members who only know green bottles.

products.menuLine: Jameson Irish Whiskey · Ireland · $28 · 80 proof (40.0% ABV) · Notes: honey, green apple

guidance.bestFor: Irish whiskey beginner, St. Patrick's crowd, green-bottle recognition.
guidance.avoidIf: User wants single malt Scotch only.
guidance.whyRecommend: Recommend when the member wants the Jameson everyone knows — honey, apple, and easy Irish malt.
guidance.memberBlurb: Jameson Original — Ireland's green bottle on every American bar. Honey and green apple malt that works neat, on ice, or beside a medium cigar.
